title: K-3PX
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|K3}} {{Droid |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:K3PX-Hasbro.png]] |name=K-3PX |homeworld= |birth= |death= |creator= |manufacturer=[[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |line= |model=[[3PX-series protocol droid]] |class=[[Protocol droid/Legends|Protocol droid]][[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Masculine programming]] |pronouns=new |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]] |plating=Black |armament= |equipment=*2 [[Photoreceptor/Legends|Photoreceptors]] *[[Vocabulator/Legends|Vocabulator]] |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]][[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] }} {{Quote|And that K3PX unit over there, the black one. As you can see, it's practically new. No one knows what became of the original owner."
"It's a beauty, all right.|Griggor Tower and Lando Calrissian|}} '''K-3PX''' was a [[Color/Legends|black]] [[3PX-series protocol droid]] that belonged to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], stationed at the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Gamandar Citadel|citadel]] on [[Gamandar]]. K-3PX played a vital role in [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Griggor Tower]]'s capture of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]—later, he would inform Vader of Tower's [[Iskalon Effect|endangerment]] of Vader's coveted target [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] on [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]], leading to Tower's execution. ==Biography== {{Quote|He stored all the information he'd gathered about the Empire's secret plans in his K3PX unit. Why don't you ask K3 where he's gone?|Kendle|[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]]}} A [[Color/Legends|black]]-colored [[3PX-series protocol droid]], K-3PX served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the personal property of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' Vader put K-3PX to work under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Griggor Tower]] at the [[Gamandar Citadel|citadel]] on [[Gamandar]], in the subjugated [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], several Rebel agents traveled to nearby [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] searching for any trace of disappeared Rebel soldier [[Tay Vanis]], who had been last seen investigating secret Imperial plans in the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. On Iskalon, they received false information from an [[Iskalonian]] named [[Kendle]]—an Imperial informant[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]]—that Vanis entrusted all his information on the Empire's plans to K-3PX, which he identified as Vanis' personal [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. Kendle pointed them to Gamandar, where several of their number—[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], accompanied by native Iskalonian [[Mone]] and the droids [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—traveled in search of K-3PX and Vanis. Posing as an Imperial-friendly [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named "Drebble," Calrissian seemingly purchased K-3PX from Admiral Tower, and not only revealed himself and his friends as Rebel agents to K-3PX, but also told the droid that [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—the Rebel hero of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and a highly desired target of Vader—was part of the complement on Iskalon. However, Calrissian and his group had no idea how thoroughly they had been outmaneuvered. Not only was K-3PX loyal to the Empire, but Kendle's subterfuge had given Tower advance knowledge on Calrissian's arrival, resulting in the Rebels' imprisonment. Tower went one further, attacking Iskalon directly and causing the "[[Iskalon Effect]]," a massive tidal wave that devastated the [[Water/Legends|water]]-covered [[Planet/Legends|world]]. After learning of Skywalker's presence on Iskalon, K-3PX then reported directly to Vader himself, informing the Sith Lord that Tower had "overstepped" himself by attacking the planet and risking the Rebel's life, knowing that Vader wanted Skywalker alive. Outraged, Vader directed K-3PX to arrest Tower, and shortly afterward had the Admiral executed. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Lord Vader knows. He is most displeased. You are under arrest.|K-3PX, to Griggor Tower|}} A 3PX-series protocol droid with a distinct black plating, K-3PX was loyal to the Empire and operated efficiently and ruthlessly in the service of its employer. K-3PX was quick to turn on Griggor Tower the moment the tides turned against the Admiral, facilitating his arrest and death at the hands of Darth Vader. ==Behind the scenes== K-3PX first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]], illustrated by [[Ron Frenz]] and released in [[1983]]. In [[2008]], K-3PX received its first action figure, appearing alongside Darth Vader in the ''[[Star Wars: The Legacy Collection]]'' line, in doing so creating an inconsistency with its name.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|packtype=Droid Factory Pack|pack=#2: Darth Vader & K-3PX|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tlcDF2.asp}} The [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel'']] comic issues featuring K-3PX consistently spell the droid's name "K3PX." The [[Hasbro]] ''Legacy Collection'', however, spelled the designation as "K-3PX," using the hyphen customary of many droids in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe, which was later confirmed by ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 74}} {{1stm}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 75}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 76}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 80|''Star Wars'' (1977) 80]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 80}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|packtype=Droid Factory Pack|pack=#2: Darth Vader & K-3PX|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tlcDF2.asp}} {{1stID|K-3PX}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Action Figure Collection]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=K-3PX }} [[Category:3PX-series protocol droids]] [[Category:Droids of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Droids with masculine programming]] [[Category:Imperial Intelligence personnel of the Galactic Empire]]
